title: senecio squalidus var. bipinnatifidus
intro: Senecio squalidus var. bipinnatifidus, also known as Jacob's Ladder or Greek Valerian, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as relieving anxiety and insomnia, reducing inflammation, and soothing digestive issues. Jacob's Ladder can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any health condition.
